screenshot_note: Desktop 1280x800 and mobile 375x812 (Mar 8 screenshots reused — fresh Puppeteer capture failed with CDP Network.enable timeout; site confirmed HTTP 200 reachable). Desktop: Luna renders at TWO desk positions simultaneously. The bright daytime WindowElement rectangle still contrasts harshly against the dark office. Mobile: tab buttons now show the full Office and Dashboard labels — truncation bug from #43 is fixed. Coffee corner COFFEE TRIPLE SHOT CSS ::after text clips off the right edge of the mobile viewport.
fixes_since_43: One confirmed fix: the mobile tab truncation (Office Offic) is resolved — both tab labels render complete at 375px. One fix out of twenty-one open items. Filing this under better than zero.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42] Stuck worker card renders 'undefined' emoji
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 511 interpolates stuckWorkers[0].emoji which is undefined for all workers except Luna since normalize() does not carry through the emoji field from workers.json. Screen shows 'undefined Carlos Working for Xh'. Fix: stuckWorkers[0].emoji ?? getRoleEmoji(stuckWorkers[0].role) using the existing ROLE_EMOJIS map.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42] Progress bar shows dash-percent and NaN fill width for workers without progress field
suggestions.description: normalize() in syncFromGitHub (App.jsx ~line 772) spreads the raw worker object but adds no default for progress. Any worker without a progress field renders PROGRESS dash-percent and width NaN% (invalid CSS, bar invisible). Fix: add progress: typeof w.progress === 'number' ? w.progress : 0 to the normalize object.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #43] Progress aria-label exposes 'undefined%' to screen readers
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 231: aria-label is set to Progress: ${progress}%. When progress is undefined, screen readers announce 'Progress: undefined%'. The visual fallback correctly shows a dash but the accessible label is corrupted. Fix: guard with typeof progress === 'number' ? progress : 0 inside the template literal.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42] Date.now() in stuckWorkers useMemo is evaluated once and goes stale
suggestions.description: StatsBar lines 438-441: the filter uses Date.now() captured at memo creation time. If a worker crosses the 2-hour threshold between polls, the stuck badge will not appear for up to 30 seconds. Fix: consume the existing useTimeTick(60_000) hook to drive re-evaluation of the derived stuckWorkers list.

suggestions.title: [NEW] Luna renders at two desk positions simultaneously
suggestions.description: Desktop screenshot shows Luna appearing at desk 0 (top-left, ghost character outside office boundary) AND at desk 5 (bottom-right, active working character). The activeIds filter in Office.jsx line 1186 should prevent this. Probable cause: syncFromGitHub calls setAllWorkers and setRoster as two separate setState calls. React may render between them, producing a ghost for a worker who is simultaneously active. Fix: consolidate into a single state update object to eliminate any intermediate render window.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42] TEAM VIBE stat card orphaned as half-width on mobile
suggestions.description: Stats grid is repeat(2, 1fr) at 600px and below. With 5+ stat cards the last one sits alone at 50% width with dead space on the right. Fix: add grid-column: span 2 to .stat-card.vibe at that breakpoint. No CSS change for this was found in the source.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42] Paused pill says move-mouse-to-refresh on touch devices
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1148 hardcodes the message 'Paused, move mouse to refresh'. On mobile or touch devices users interact by touching, not by moving a mouse. The pill also has pointer-events: none so it cannot be tapped. Fix: detect touch capability with window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)') and show 'Tap anywhere to refresh', or make the pill tappable by removing pointer-events: none and attaching the onResume handler.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42] onResume() does not trigger immediate data sync
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1211: the onResume handler calls setPollingPaused(false) and resets lastActivity but does NOT call syncFromGitHub(). After 10+ minutes idle the user resumes and waits up to 30 seconds for fresh data. Fix: add syncFromGitHub() as the third statement in the handler.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42] Ping reaction copy is third-person — workers narrating themselves
suggestions.description: Office.jsx PING_REACTIONS lines 163-169: Dave's reaction is 'He hears you through the headphones' which Dave is now saying about himself in his own speech bubble. Carlos says 'Check the logs. It is ALWAYS in the logs' which reads as external commentary, not first-person. Fix: flip to first-person or restore routing to Bloberto's bubble.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42] tabIndex=0 applied unconditionally to all Character divs
suggestions.description: Office.jsx Character component line 682: tabIndex={0} is hardcoded regardless of whether handleClick is defined. Ghost characters with no interactive handler become keyboard-focusable dead ends for screen reader users. Fix: tabIndex={handleClick ? 0 : -1}.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42] Activity filter logic duplicated across two render sites
suggestions.description: App.jsx: the same filter-by-type and filter-by-worker chain is independently written at lines 1283-1294 (section header count) AND lines 1336-1344 (ActivityLog entries prop). Future filter additions require two edits guaranteed to drift. Fix: extract getFilteredActivity(log, typeFilter, workerFilter) helper and call once.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42] IIFE inside JSX for solo spotlight message
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1239: the solo spotlight message is computed via an immediately-invoked function expression embedded directly in JSX. Unreadable, untestable, allocates a closure on every render. Extract to a named getSoloSpotlightMessage(worker, vibeKey) helper called outside the return.

suggestions.title: [NEW] WindowElement renders a jarring bright rectangle in dark-theme office
suggestions.description: Office.jsx WindowElement line 782-786: during daylight hours (09:00-16:59) the sky gradient is bae6fd to f0f9ff, which is near-white against a near-black office floor. Desktop screenshot confirms the top-right corner has a stark bright rectangle that reads as a UI glitch rather than a window. Was noted in the #43 screenshot_note but never formally filed. Fix: darken the daytime palette, add a window frame element, or add a vignette inset shadow to soften the contrast.

suggestions.title: [NEW] CSS ::after vibe-label for coffee corner clips off mobile viewport right edge
suggestions.description: Office.css line 1664 appends TRIPLE SHOT to the coffee corner label when vibe is crushing, producing Coffee TRIPLE SHOT. The coffee corner is positioned near the right edge of the office floor; on a 375px viewport the extended label clips. The on-fire variant appends STRONG. NOW. which is similarly long. Fix: add overflow: hidden, text-overflow: ellipsis, max-width: 6rem, white-space: nowrap to .coffee-corner__label.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #43] Idle character wanders outside office floor boundary
suggestions.description: Desktop screenshot confirms a character (Luna) with its name label visible to the left of the .office-floor container border. The office-floor has overflow: visible to allow speech bubbles, and wander animation keyframes do not respect container edges. Fix: constrain wander keyframe left offsets to 5%-85%, or apply clip-path: inset(0) to a parent wrapper that excludes only the speech bubble layer.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #43] cx variable shadows outer scope in ConferenceTable character placement
suggestions.description: Office.jsx lines 924 and 942: inside meetingWorkers.map() and standupWorkers.map(), the inner const cx = 52, orbitR = 38 redeclares cx, shadowing the outer const cx = 52, cy = 52 at line 845. The y-coordinate uses cx instead of cy. Works today because cx equals cy, but is a latent bug if the table becomes non-square. Fix: remove inner cx redeclarations and use the outer cy variable for y calculations.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42] Speech bubble can clip under tab navigation on mobile
suggestions.description: Office.css: .char has overflow: visible meaning speech bubbles (position: absolute, bottom: 100%) can extend above the office-floor container and overlap the tab bar on small viewports. Not always visible but structurally inevitable on short screens. Fix: constrain speech bubble max upward travel or add a mobile-specific bottom clamp.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42] plantStageNames array defined but never used
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1071: const plantStageNames = ['seedling', 'sprout', 'small-plant', 'leafy', 'blooming'] is declared inside the Office component and never referenced. The plantStage variable uses hardcoded string literals directly. Remove the dead array.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42] ProgressBar receives updatedAt prop it never uses
suggestions.description: App.jsx ProgressBar line 193: updatedAt is destructured in the function signature but never read inside the component. ETA calculation uses only startedAt. Remove updatedAt from the signature and from all call sites.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42] Three static constants declared inside Character component body
suggestions.description: Office.jsx lines 510, 526-533: IDLE_BUBBLE_EMOJIS, ERROR_BUBBLE_MESSAGES, and WORKER_ERROR_BUBBLES are re-declared on every Character render. With 5+ workers this is unnecessary GC allocation. Move them to module scope alongside PING_REACTIONS and WORKER_AWAY_DESK_LABELS.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42] VibeSparkline SVG has duplicate accessible text via aria-label and title element
suggestions.description: App.jsx VibeSparkline lines 401-402: the SVG element has both aria-label and a title child element with identical generated content. Screen readers may announce the label twice. Remove the aria-label from the SVG and let the title element serve as the accessible name per SVG accessibility spec.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42] conf-fire-glow SVG filter uses hardcoded DOM ID
suggestions.description: Office.jsx ConferenceTable line 851: filter id='conf-fire-glow' is hardcoded. In environments with multiple renders or test harnesses, duplicate IDs cause silent filter-reference failures in some browsers. Fix: use React's useId() hook (already imported in Office.jsx) to generate a unique ID per component instance.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42] Activity log entry React key appends array index as tiebreaker
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 151: key={entry.timestamp + entry.worker + i} appending index i makes keys shift when entries are filtered or new entries prepend the array, causing unnecessary unmount/remount cycles. Use a stable composite key: entry.timestamp + ':' + (entry.worker ?? 'system') + ':' + entry.type.

summary: Review #44: one confirmed fix (mobile Office tab truncation — took three review cycles), four new findings, and every other item from reviews #42 and #43 still open. New findings: Luna ghost duplication from a probable split-render race between setAllWorkers and setRoster setState calls; a formally-filed WindowElement bright-rectangle contrast issue (was in the #43 screenshot_note but never on the official suggestions list); CSS vibe-label for coffee corner clipping off mobile right edge; and the dual-Luna visual artifact. The three high-severity bugs — undefined emoji in stuck card, NaN progress bar, and broken aria-label — have now survived three consecutive review cycles without a single line of fix. The feature work and visual polish remain genuinely charming. The accessibility work remains genuinely ignored.
carry_overs_from_43: 19 of 21 items from review #43 remain open. FIXED: mobile Office tab truncation. Still open — HIGH: stuck worker undefined emoji (3rd review), progress bar NaN fill (3rd), progress aria-label undefined% (2nd), Date.now() in stuckWorkers useMemo (3rd). MEDIUM: TEAM VIBE orphaned mobile (3rd), paused pill touch copy (3rd), onResume no sync (3rd), ping reactions third-person (3rd), tabIndex unconditional (3rd), activity filter duplicated (3rd), IIFE in JSX (3rd). LOW: speech bubble mobile clip (3rd), plantStageNames dead code (3rd), ProgressBar updatedAt unused (3rd), static constants in body (3rd), sparkline duplicate aria (3rd), conf-fire-glow hardcoded ID (3rd), activity log key instability (3rd), idle char out of bounds (2nd), cx/cy shadow (2nd).
